title: Question: SystemVerilog nettype support ?
body:
---


Author Name: **j briquet**
Original Redmine Message: 1782 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi all,

Does verilator support resolution function as available in SystemVerilog ?
I mean I have tried to verilate this code as follow :

```
typedef real realsum;

function automatic Tsum(input realsum driver [3]);

Tsum =0.0;

for( int i=0; i<3; i=i+1) 
    Tsum += driver[i];

endfunction

nettype T wTsum with Tsum; // this line error


```

But Verilator have displayed these following error message :
```
syntax error, unexpected IDENTIFIER
Unsupported: SystemVerilog 2005 reserved word not implemented: with

```

When using the option +systemverilogext+sv (for getting the feature from SV-2012) , I have got this :
```
Unsupported: SystemVerilog 2012 reserved word not implemented: nettype
Unsupported: SystemVerilog 2005 reserved word not implemented: with

```

Will Verilator support this syntax soon ? This resolution function are very important when modeling in SystemVerilog for Mixed-Signal ICs, because you can have 2 blocks or more that drive one net,
when using the resolution function, you can define a rule which specify how to resolve the resultant net.
It could be great for translating large mixed-signal ICs in software model :) 

Best regards and merry xmas !
